title: Compute FFT bin center frequency
description: Convert a bin index into its center frequency using the sample rate and transform length.
body: Use when interpreting which physical frequency an FFT bin represents. Do not forget that bins above N over two represent negative frequencies in a real-input transform. Actions: multiply the bin index by sample rate divided by N, verify a known tone lands at its predicted index, and record the Nyquist bin position. Validation signals include a test tone appearing at the computed bin and symmetry of the spectrum about Nyquist for real input. Risk boundary: misreading negative-frequency bins as high positive frequencies corrupts measurements, so flag any peak above the Nyquist bin for sign interpretation.
